note: Phase 1 seed covering yamllint's full public rule set (23 rules as of v1.38.0, Jan 2026). yamllint emits a 'parsable' format (file:line:col: [level] message (rule-name)) that is stdlib-regex-parseable without any JSON dependency. yamllint has no security-framed rules; the security_defer_to_reaper bucket is present but empty by design. YAML secrets/IaC-security findings live in Reaper's R3 via semgrep-community and Trivy, never yamllint.

correctness_m1.description: YAML structural-failure candidates — duplicates that will silently collapse under parsing, truthy values that round-trip wrong across parsers, empty-values that produce null where a value was expected. M1 widens on document-level key-set invariants; M5 can confirm parse outcome when a Python stdlib yaml.safe_load is available.

idiom_m7.description: YAML idiom — prefer the canonical marker, quoting discipline, and document boundaries. Routed to M7 (Idiom-fit).

complexity_m7.description: Structural-shape complexity — line-length and empty-lines as readability / git-diff-size proxies. Routed to M7 (Simplicity).

naming_m7.description: Key ordering and identifier conventions. Routed to M7 (Clarity).

testability_m7.description: Phase 2 gap — yamllint does not surface test-fixture-specific shape checks. Seeded empty.

security_defer_to_reaper.description: yamllint ships no security-framed rules. Bucket present for schema-compliance; empty by design. Reaper R3 owns YAML-secrets and IaC-misconfiguration taxonomy via semgrep + Trivy.
